tool.name: SameStr Convert
tool.description: Convert MetaPhlAn or mOTUS marker alignments to nucleotide variant profiles
tool.command:
mkdir -p out_convert/ &&
cp '$alignment' 'file_0.bam' && cp '$profile' 'file_0.profile.txt' &&
samestr convert --input-files 'file_0.bam' --marker-dir '$database.fields.path' --output-dir out_convert/ --min-vcov '$alignment_param.min_vcov' --min-aln-identity '$alignment_param.min_aln_identity' --min-aln-len '$alignment_param.min_aln_len' --min-base-qual '$alignment_param.min_base_qual' --min-aln-qual '$alignment_param.min_aln_qual'

param.name: database
param.label: SameStr database
param.help: SameStr database built from MetaPhlAn or mOTUS markers

param.label: Minimum alignment quality
param.help: Increasing this threshold can drastically reduce the number of considered variants.

tool.help:
**SameStr Convert**
SameStr Convert processes marker gene alignments from taxonomic profiling tools (MetaPhlAn or mOTUS) to nucleotide variant profiles for strain-level analysis. It outputs per-clade variant profiles that can be used in downstream SameStr workflow steps.
**Input Requirements**
The following files must be provided: - A file in BAM (.bam) format containing marker alignments generated by MetaPhlAn or mOTUS. - A taxonomic profile file (.profile.txt) corresponding to the alignment.
**Alignment Parameters**
- Marker database. A MetaPhlAn or mOTUS clade marker database which matches the version used to generate the input alignments must be selected. - Percent identity. Minimum percent identity between read and marker sequence. Alignments with lower identity are filtered out. - Minimum alignment length. Minimum number of aligned bases required. Shorter alignments are discarded.
**Results and Outputs**
The tool generates three main outputs: - Command log (JSON format). A detailed execution log of the conversion process. - Alignment statistics (".aln_stats.txt"). Contains a summary table with statistics for each processed alignment. - Clade NPZ files. A collection of compressed NumPy files (.npz). Each file contains the nucleotide variant profile for a specific clade.
